records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2619 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a Muhrim changes his clothes. However, when he enters Makkah, he should wear the two garments of Ihram that he entered Ihram with. It is disliked to sell them."
It has also been narrated that there is a concession allowing their sale.
records.arabicText:
2619 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنَّ يُغَيِّرَ اَلْمُحْرِمُ ثِيَابَهُ وَ لَكِنْ إِذَا دَخَلَ مَكَّةَ لَبِسَ ثَوْبَيْ إِحْرَامِهِ اَللَّذَيْنِ أَحْرَمَ فِيهِمَا وَ كُرِهَ أَنْ يَبِيعَهُمَا ».
وقد رويت رخصة في بيعهما.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2619 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no harm if a Muhrim changes his clothes. However, when he enters Makkah, he should wear the two garments of Ihram that he entered Ihram with. It is disliked to sell them."
It has also been narrated that there is a concession allowing their sale.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2620 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
"I dislike for a Muhrim to sleep on a yellow mattress or a yellow cushion."
records.arabicText: 2620 - وَ رَوَى أَبُو بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ يَقُولُ : «أَكْرَهُ أَنْ يَنَامَ اَلْمُحْرِمُ عَلَى اَلْفِرَاشِ اَلْأَصْفَرِ أَوِ اَلْمِرْفَقَةِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2620 - Abu Basir narrated from Abu Ja'far (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "I dislike for a Muhrim to sleep on a yellow mattress or a yellow cushion."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2621 - Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a Muhrim wearing khazz (a type of fabric).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2621 - وَ سَأَلَ عَبْدُ اَلرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ اَلْحَجَّاجِ أَبَا اَلْحَسَنِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَلْبَسُ اَلْخَزَّ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ بِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2621 - Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Abu al-Hasan (as) about a Muhrim wearing khazz (a type of fabric).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2622 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"If a Muhrim fears for his safety, he may wear weapons."
records.arabicText: 2622 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْمُحْرِمُ إِذَا خَافَ لَبِسَ اَلسِّلاَحَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2622 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a Muhrim fears for his safety, he may wear weapons."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2623 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him about a Muhrim who needs to wear various types of different clothing.
Imam (as) said: "For each type of clothing, he must offer an expiation (fidya)."
records.arabicText: 2623 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ إِذَا اِحْتَاجَ إِلَى ضُرُوبٍ مِنَ اَلثِّيَابِ مُخْتَلِفَةٍ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ «عَلَيْهِ لِكُلِّ صِنْفٍ مِنْهَا فِدَاءٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2623 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from one of the Imams (as), who said:
I asked him about a Muhrim who needs to wear various types of different clothing.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "For each type of clothing, he must offer an expiation (fidya)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2624 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a Muhrim whose garment is affected by janabah (ritual impurity).
Imam (as) said: "He should not wear it until he washes it, and his state of Ihram remains valid."
records.arabicText: 2624 - وَ رَوَى مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ عَمَّارٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ تُصِيبُ ثَوْبَهُ اَلْجَنَابَةُ قَالَ «لاَ يَلْبَسْهُ حَتَّى يَغْسِلَهُ وَ إِحْرَامُهُ تَامٌّ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2624 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a Muhrim whose garment is affected by janabah (ritual impurity).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "He should not wear it until he washes it, and his state of Ihram remains valid."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2625 - In a narration from Hammad ibn Uthman, from Hariz, Abu Abdullah (as) said:
"A woman in Ihram should let her garment hang over her face down to her chin."
records.arabicText: 2625 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ عَنْ حَرِيزٍ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : «اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ تَسْدُلُ اَلثَّوْبَ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا إِلَى اَلذَّقَنِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2625 - In a narration from Hammad ibn Uthman, from Hariz, Abu Abdullah (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman in Ihram should let her garment hang over her face down to her chin."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2626 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, from Imam (as), he said:
"A woman should let her garment hang over her face from above down to her chest when she is riding."
records.arabicText: 2626 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ مُعَاوِيَةَ بْنِ عَمَّارٍ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «تَسْدُلُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلثَّوْبَ عَلَى وَجْهِهَا مِنْ أَعْلاَهَا إِلَى اَلنَّحْرِ إِذَا كَانَتْ رَاكِبَةً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2626 - In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, from Imam (as), he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman should let her garment hang over her face from above down to her chest when she is riding."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2627 - Abdullah ibn Maymun narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq, from his father (as), who said:
"A woman in Ihram should not wear a face veil, because the Ihram of a woman is in her face, while the Ihram of a man is in his head."
records.arabicText: 2627 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ مَيْمُونٍ عَنِ اَلصَّادِقِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ لاَ تَتَنَقَّبُ لِأَنَّ إِحْرَامَ اَلْمَرْأَةِ فِي وَجْهِهَا وَ إِحْرَامَ اَلرَّجُلِ فِي رَأْسِهِ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2627 - Abdullah ibn Maymun narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq, from his father (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman in Ihram should not wear a face veil, because the Ihram of a woman is in her face, while the Ihram of a man is in his head."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.2628 - Abu Ja'far (as) passed by a woman in Ihram who had covered her face with a fan. He moved the fan away from her face with his staff.
records.arabicText: 2628 - وَ مَرَّ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : بِامْرَأَةٍ مُحْرِمَةٍ قَدِ اِسْتَتَرَتْ بِمِرْوَحَةٍ فَأَمَاطَ اَلْمِرْوَحَةَ بِقَضِيبِهِ عَنْ وَجْهِهَا.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2628 - Abu Ja'far (as) passed by
records.thaqalaynMatn: a woman in Ihram who had covered her face with a fan. He moved the fan away from her face with his staff.

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2629 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"A woman in Ihram who is menstruating may wear an inner garment (ghilalah) under her clothes."
records.arabicText: 2629 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «تَلْبَسُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ اَلْحَائِضُ تَحْتَ ثِيَابِهَا غِلاَلَةً ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2629 - Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman in Ihram who is menstruating may wear an inner garment (ghilalah) under her clothes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2630 - Yahya ibn Abi al-Ala narrated from Abu Abdullah, from his father (as):
"He disliked the use of a niqab (face veil) and gloves for a woman in Ihram."
records.arabicText: 2630 - وَ رَوَى يَحْيَى بْنُ أَبِي اَلْعَلاَءِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : أَنَّهُ كَرِهَ لِلْمُحْرِمَةِ اَلْبُرْقُعَ وَ اَلْقُفَّازَيْنِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2630 - Yahya ibn Abi al-Ala narrated from Abu Abdullah, from his father (as):
records.thaqalaynMatn: "He disliked the use of a niqab (face veil) and gloves for a woman in Ihram."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2631 - Muhammad ibn Ali al-Halabi asked Imam (as) about a woman in Ihram: "Can she wear trousers (sarawil)?"
Imam (as) replied: "Yes, as she intends by that to maintain modesty."
records.arabicText: 2631 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ اَلْحَلَبِيُّ : عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ إِذَا أَحْرَمَتْ أَتَلْبَسُ اَلسَّرَاوِيلَ؟ فَقَالَ: «نَعَمْ إِنَّمَا تُرِيدُ بِذَلِكَ اَلسَّتْرَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2631 - Muhammad ibn Ali al-Halabi asked Imam (as) about a woman in Ihram: "Can she wear trousers (sarawil)?"
Imam (as) replied:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes, as she intends by that to maintain modesty."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2632 - Al-Kahili narrated from Imam (as) that he said:
"A woman in Ihram may wear all types of jewelry except conspicuous earrings (al-qurt al-mashhoor) and a conspicuous necklace (al-qiladah al-mashhoorah)."
records.arabicText: 2632 - وَ رَوَى اَلْكَاهِلِيُّ عَنْهُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ : «تَلْبَسُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ اَلْحُلِيَّ كُلَّهُ إِلاَّ اَلْقُرْطَ اَلْمَشْهُورَ وَ اَلْقِلاَدَةَ اَلْمَشْهُورَةَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2632 - Al-Kahili narrated from Imam (as) that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A woman in Ihram may wear all types of jewelry except conspicuous earrings (al-qurt al-mashhoor) and a conspicuous necklace (al-qiladah al-mashhoorah)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2633 - Amir ibn Jutha'ah asked Imam (as) about dyed garments worn by a woman in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it, except for prominently dyed garments (al-mufdam al-mashhoor)."
records.arabicText: 2633 - وَ سَأَلَهُ عَامِرُ بْنُ جُذَاعَةَ : عَنْ مُصَبَّغَاتِ اَلثِّيَابِ تَلْبَسُهَا اَلْمَرْأَةُ اَلْمُحْرِمَةُ فَقَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ إِلاَّ اَلْمُفْدَمُ اَلْمَشْهُورُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2633 - Amir ibn Jutha'ah asked Imam (as) about dyed garments worn by a woman in Ihram.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm in it, except for prominently dyed garments (al-mufdam al-mashhoor)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2634 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a woman in Ihram:
"She may wear all types of jewellery, except for jewellery that is prominently worn for adornment."
records.arabicText: 2634 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي اَلْمُحْرِمَةِ أَنَّهَا تَلْبَسُ اَلْحُلِيَّ كُلَّهُ إِلاَّ حُلِيّاً مَشْهُوراً لِزِينَةٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2634 - Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a woman in Ihram:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "She may wear all types of jewellery, except for jewellery that is prominently worn for adornment."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2635 - Sama'ah asked Imam (as) about a woman in Ihram wearing silk.
Imam (as) said: "It is not proper for her to wear pure silk without any mixture. However, there is no harm if she wears khazz (a silk-blended fabric) or a garment with embroidery while she is in Ihram. If a man passes by her, she should cover herself with her garment but not use her hand to shield herself from the sun. She may wear khazz, although people may say that khazz contains silk. What is disliked is pure silk."
records.arabicText: 2635 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَمَاعَةُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمَةِ تَلْبَسُ اَلْحَرِيرَ فَقَالَ «لاَ يَصْلُحُ لَهَا أَنْ تَلْبَسَ حَرِيراً مَحْضاً لاَ خِلْطَ فِيهِ فَأَمَّا اَلْخَزُّ وَ اَلْعَلَمُ فِي اَلثَّوْبِ فَلاَ بَأْسَ بِأَنْ تَلْبَسَهُ وَ هِيَ مُحْرِمَةٌ وَ إِنْ مَرَّ بِهَا رَجُلٌ اِسْتَتَرَتْ مِنْهُ بِثَوْبِهَا وَ لاَ تَسْتُرُ بِيَدِهَا مِنَ اَلشَّمْسِ وَ تَلْبَسُ اَلْخَزَّ أَمَا إِنَّهُمْ سَيَقُولُونَ إِنَّ فِي اَلْخَزِّ حَرِيراً وَ إِنَّمَا يُكْرَهُ اَلْحَرِيرُ اَلْمُبْهَمُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2635 - Sama'ah asked Imam (as) about a woman in Ihram wearing silk.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "It is not proper for her to wear pure silk without any mixture. However, there is no harm if she wears khazz (a silk-blended fabric) or a garment with embroidery while she is in Ihram. If a man passes by her, she should cover herself with her garment but not use her hand to shield herself from the sun. She may wear khazz, although people may say that khazz contains silk. What is disliked is pure silk."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2636 - Abu Basir al-Muradi asked Imam (as) about a woman wearing qazz (a fabric made from silk and other materials) while in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it. What is disliked is pure silk (al-harir al-mubham)."
records.arabicText: 2636 - وَ سَأَلَهُ أَبُو بَصِيرٍ اَلْمُرَادِيُّ : عَنِ اَلْقَزِّ تَلْبَسُهُ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فِي اَلْإِحْرَامِ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ إِنَّمَا يُكْرَهُ اَلْحَرِيرُ اَلْمُبْهَمُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2636 - Abu Basir al-Muradi asked Imam (as) about a woman wearing qazz (a fabric made from silk and other materials) while in Ihram.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it. What is disliked is pure silk (al-harir al-mubham)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2637 - Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Imam (as) about a woman wearing jewelry while in Ihram.
Imam (as) said: "She may wear anklets (masak) and bracelets (khalkhalayn)."
records.arabicText: 2637 - وَ سَأَلَهُ يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ : عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تَلْبَسُ اَلْحُلِيَّ قَالَ «تَلْبَسُ اَلْمَسَكَ وَ اَلْخَلْخَالَيْنِ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2637 - Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Imam (as) about a woman wearing jewelry while in Ihram.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "She may wear anklets (masak) and bracelets (khalkhalayn)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2638 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
"There is no harm if a woman enters Ihram wearing gold or khazz (a silk-blended fabric). What is disliked is only pure silk (al-harir al-mubham)."
records.arabicText: 2638 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَلَبِيُّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : «لاَ بَأْسَ أَنْ تُحْرِمَ اَلْمَرْأَةُ فِي اَلذَّهَبِ وَ اَلْخَزِّ وَ لَيْسَ يُكْرَهُ إِلاَّ اَلْحَرِيرُ اَلْمَحْضُ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2638 - Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm if a woman enters Ihram wearing gold or khazz (a silk-blended fabric). What is disliked is only pure silk (al-harir al-mubham)."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2639 - In a narration from Hariz, he said:
"If a woman has jewelry that she did not acquire specifically for Ihram, she does not need to remove her jewelry."
records.arabicText: 2639 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ حَرِيزٍ قَالَ : «إِذَا كَانَ لِلْمَرْأَةِ حُلِيٌّ لَمْ تُحْدِثْهُ لِلْإِحْرَامِ لَمْ تَنْزِعْ حُلِيَّهَا».
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.2639 - In a narration from Hariz, he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If a woman has jewelry that she did not acquire specifically for Ihram, she does not need to remove her jewelry."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2640 - It was narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Nahdi, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked, while I was present, about a woman entering Ihram wearing a turban (imamah) with an emblem (alam).
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."
records.arabicText: 2640 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلنَّهْدِيِّ قَالَ : سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ أَنَا حَاضِرٌ عَنِ اَلْمَرْأَةِ تُحْرِمُ فِي اَلْعِمَامَةِ وَ لَهَا عَلَمٌ قَالَ «لاَ بَأْسَ ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2640 - It was narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Nahdi, who said:
Abu Abdullah (as) was asked, while I was present, about a woman entering Ihram wearing a turban (imamah) with an emblem (alam).
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "There is no harm in it."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2641 - Sa'id al-A'raj asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim tying his loincloth (izar) around his neck.
Imam (as) said: "No."
records.arabicText: 2641 - وَ سَأَلَهُ سَعِيدٌ اَلْأَعْرَجُ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَعْقِدُ إِزَارَهُ فِي عُنُقِهِ قَالَ «لاَ».

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2642 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim placing the strap of a water bag (qirbah) on his head while drawing water.
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 2642 - وَ سَأَلَهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُسْلِمٍ : عَنِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَضَعُ عِصَامَ اَلْقِرْبَةِ عَلَى رَأْسِهِ إِذَا اِسْتَقَى فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ».
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.2642 - Muhammad ibn Muslim asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim placing the strap of a water bag (qirbah) on his head while drawing water.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Yes."

records.category: Book of Pilgrimage and Its Virtues
records.chapter: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible to Wear in the State of Ihram
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.2643 - Ya'qub ibn Shu'ayb asked Imam (as) about a Muhrim who has a wound. Can he bind it or wrap it with a cloth?
Imam (as) said: "Yes."
records.arabicText: 2643 - وَ سَأَلَهُ يَعْقُوبُ بْنُ شُعَيْبٍ : عَنِ اَلرَّجُلِ اَلْمُحْرِمِ يَكُونُ بِهِ اَلْقَرْحَةُ يَرْبِطُهَا أَوْ يُعَصِّبُهَا بِخِرْقَةٍ فَقَالَ «نَعَمْ».
